content: Remote OK Maintenance &amp; MEP Technician Scales and Associates <p><strong>Maintenance &amp; MEP Technician â Luxury Hospitality Operations (The Bahamas)</strong></p><p><br></p><p>We are supporting a prestigious private island hospitality operation in The Bahamas in the search for a Maintenance &amp; MEP Technician. This is a hands-on role suited to an experienced maintenance professional with strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to safely complete a wide range of in-house repairs while recognising when specialist support is required.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li>Carry out daily, preventive, and scheduled maintenance across guest areas, back-of-house facilities, kitchens, docks, utility spaces, and technical plant rooms.</li><li>Perform practical repairs, including minor plumbing, basic electrical fault-finding, carpentry, painting, sealing, and repairs to doors, fixtures, fittings, and hardware.</li><li>Conduct routine inspections and basic maintenance of MEP systems, including pumps, valves, filters, HVAC equipment, water supply, drainage, and wastewater systems.</li><li>Support specialist contractors during servicing and repairs of generators, reverse osmosis (RO) water systems, wastewater and sewage systems, HVAC, and electrical infrastructure.</li><li>Maintain accurate maintenance logs, work orders, inventory of tools and spare parts, and ensure compliance with PPE requirements and safe working practices at all times.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Requirements</strong></p><ul><li>3+ years of hands-on maintenance experience within a hotel, resort, restaurant, facilities management, or MEP environment.</li><li>Practical skills in plumbing, basic electrical maintenance, carpentry, and general building repairs.</li><li>Working knowledge of pumps, drainage, water systems, and safe fault identification and reporting procedures.</li><li>A relevant trade certification or MEP qualification is advantageous.</li><li>Physically fit and capable of working in an island environment, including vessel transfers, lifting, and working at height.</li><li>Flexible and willing to work weekends, public holidays, and special events as operationally required.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Preferred Experience</strong></p><ul><li>Previous experience in hospitality or resort maintenance.</li><li>Experience working in a private island or remote island environment.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Please note: We are only able to consider applicants who have the legal right to work in The Bahamas.</strong></p> engineer, sys admin, ops Nassau, PGIM Ireland <strong>Location: Letterkenny - Hybrid 2 days p/week onsite<br><br></strong><strong>What You Will Do<br><br></strong>Working in the PGIM People Team, we are looking for a collaborative, inquisitive and analytical professional like you to fulfill the role of Manager, Compensation &amp; Analytics within PGIM Total Rewards.<br><br>You will have the opportunity to work with colleagues from around the world and gain exposure to compensation, HR data and practices across different geographies. You will envision and create innovative solutions in support of PGIMâs dynamic business strategy as well as manage established compensation processes.<br><br>Responsibilities include working with internal partners and clients to execute initiatives and creatively solve PGIM people-related compensation challenges for assigned areas.<br><br>You will develop methods to manage headcount and compensation reporting analytics, compensation expense forecasting and the year-end compensation process. You will also develop and maintain compensation structures based on benchmarking insights and support People Team partners in compensation advisory matters.<br><br>The ideal candidate will partner with the wider PGIM and Prudential Total Rewards teams, People Business Leaders, People Operations and PGIM Finance to design, streamline, and execute a wide variety of Total Reward processes, analytics, and forecasts as well as manage ad-hoc requests.<br><br>This role will work closely with colleagues across the globe, and flexibility will be required to support the teamâs priorities.<br><br><strong>What You Can Expect<br><br></strong><ul><li>Partner with People Business Leaders, People Operations, and PGIM Finance to:</li><ul><li>Develop and oversee a streamlined approach for headcount and compensation expense analytics including accruals, modeling and forecasting for investment professional, sales and corporate staff.</li><li>Manage and execute year-end compensation processes for assigned PGIM areas including compensation (salary, short-term and long-term incentives), promotions and performance management.</li></ul><li>Advise People Team and business leaders on impacts of EU Pay Transparency Directive and maintain structures that support continued compliance with our regulatory obligations.</li><li>Manage ongoing market data matching and job architecture, ensuring accuracy and consistency, and deliver guidance and insights to colleagues on our grade and pay structures. </li><li>Manage ad-hoc requests and provide day-to-day compensation consulting support to PGIM Total Rewards and the broader People Team.<br></li></ul><strong>What You Will Bring<br><br></strong><ul><li>Curiosity and creativeness: developing innovative approaches to solving compensation related challenges and improve ways of working. </li><li>Excellent analytical skills and attention to detail; the ability to tell a story and derive insight from analysis to impact business decisions.</li><li>Technical skills:</li><ul><li>Strong Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, SUMIFs, Nesting, Power Pivot Tables, Charts)</li><li>Exposure to Power BI, Visier and/or other data analytics/visualization tools and willingness to increase skill level</li><li>Developed AI promoting skills that support the continued use of Microsoft Co-Pilot</li></ul><li>Ability to prioritize and simultaneously execute on multiple projects with varied deadlines</li><li>Strong relationship-building, partnering, influencing, interpersonal and collaborative skills</li><li>Excellent work ethic and self-motivated; able to manage work in a high-volume and fast paced environment, juggle multiple priorities and work flexibly to extended hours as needed.</li><li>Experience of working with Aon and/or WTW benchmarking data.</li><li>6-8 years of experience in HR Compensation roles. </li><li>Bachelorâs Degree<br></li></ul><strong>What will set you apart?<br><br></strong><ul><li>Financial services industry, asset management, and/or global experience</li><li>Experience with position management tools/systems</li><li>Experience with Workday and compensation management tools</li><li>Experience with Microsoft Access and Microsoft PowerPoint<br><br></li></ul&g
